name: Explicit Actionability and Modal Disclosure Targets
purpose: Define explicit click targets, modal disclosure targets, urgency meter rules, and metadata visibility rules without broad text-scrubbing patches.
doctrine: If it needs executive action, it must route. If it is proof detail, it opens on demand. If it is metadata, it lives in Settings. If it is urgent, the urgency bar must show red/yellow/green with a marker. If it is a drilldown, it must drill down.
metadata_visibility_rule: Build labels, version labels, governance metadata, QA language, prototype boundary language, and internal build status belong in Settings/admin metadata and repo documentation only, not executive-facing tabs.
progressive_disclosure_rule: Primary tabs show conclusions, posture, risk, evidence gaps, and next action. Trace objects, report previews, raw scaffolds, model internals, registries, and export previews open through buttons and modals.

route_targets.message: Evidence opened to show what needs verification, what is missing, and who owns the next step.
Evidence opened to show missing proof, assumptions, and verification requirements.
Proof Pack opened to show decision records and defensibility context.
Architecture opened to show model trace, score contract, and trust model doctrine.
Settings opened because governance, boundaries, versions, and prototype metadata belong there.
Evidence opened to show healthcare and clinical-adjacent data verification requirements.
Runtime opened to show allowed action, constraints, escalation, and next human step.
Proof Pack opened to show report preview and defensibility context.

urgency_meter.color_rule: Always render red/yellow/green, with no gray segment.

boundary: Static advisory UI hardening only. This registry does not perform live scoring, live evidence retrieval, workflow automation, ticketing, notifications, backend persistence, enforcement, statistical validation, CMMC certification, healthcare compliance validation, or live integrations.
